mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.14% is at the effective lower bound. Persistently low policy rates compress discount rates applied to equity cash flows, supporting elevated valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains at or below 0.25% and the S&P 500 20-day forward return is negative in more than half of the evaluation periods, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0% signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE triggers the condition but S&P 500 60-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of occurrences, or the average return is not statistically greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth (RSAFS/CPIAUCSL) is the true measure of consumption demand. When nominal sales rise faster than inflation, real purchasing power increases, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the ratio RSAFS/CPIAUCSL rises over 3 months but the 20-day forward return of SPX is negative, or the rank IC of the ratio is below 0.05 over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production weakens (INDPRO growth below 0.5% monthly) while inflation accelerates (CPIAUCSL above 3% YoY), it signals a stagflationary supply shock that depresses corporate margins and equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%
failure_condition: If the trigger condition occurs and the 20-day forward return of SPX is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and economic momentum. This steady positive signal supports equity prices as it forecasts higher future earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases month-over-month but the 20-day forward return of SPX is negative in more than 55% of cases, or the Spearman rank correlation is below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surging above a threshold of 1,500k annualized units signals strong residential investment and consumer confidence, translating into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the economy gains momentum.
failure_condition: If HOUST exceeds 1500 but the 60-day S&P 500 forward return is negative in more than one-third of cases, or the average excess return over baseline is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that eventually forces wages higher, compressing corporate profit margins and weighing on equity prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive and UNRATE declining but the 60-day SPX return is positive in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction rank IC is below -0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, strong GDP growth combined with an accommodative federal funds rate below 2% creates a Goldilocks environment for equities: accelerating demand with low financing costs, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > 2% annualized and FEDFUNDS < 2%
failure_condition: If the trigger condition is met but the 60-day forward return of SPX is negative in more than 40% of instances, or the average return is not significantly above the unconditional mean.
